story_assessments.story_title: Bookkeeping AI is moving into the workflow, not the side window
story_assessments.target_audience: small business owners, accountants, bookkeepers, and operations leads
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want faster document intake and less manual entry without losing control over what gets posted into accounting.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Xero said its JAX platform is adding AI-powered document capture and workflow automation and that it serves 5 million customers worldwide.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The story becomes interesting when the workflow handoff is visible: AI prepares the draft, then a human approves the posting.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Shows whether AI can reduce repeatable bookkeeping steps while keeping finance control intact.
story_assessments.proof_point: Xero said its JAX agentic platform is adding AI-powered features for small businesses, accountants and bookkeepers, including document capture and workflow automation, and said it serves 5 million customers worldwide.
search_phrases: Xero JAX document capture workflow automation
story_assessments.score_reason: Very strong fit for small-business workflow audiences because the benefit is concrete, the process question is easy to understand, and the verified fact maps cleanly to a practical test.

story_assessments.story_title: AI meeting notes are becoming an admin control
story_assessments.target_audience: team admins, managers, operations leaders, and small business operators
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want control over when AI notes appear, who owns the review step, and how meeting outputs become work.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Google said admins can configure Meet AI note-taking so it only applies to meetings with three or more people.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The story turns once the setting is framed as a policy decision instead of a personal convenience feature.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Shows how to turn meeting AI into a standard operating rule with a clear review point.
story_assessments.proof_point: Google said admins can now configure AI note-taking in Meet so it can be enabled only for meetings with three or more people, with rollout and default-setting details by plan.
search_phrases: Google Meet take notes for me settings admins
Meet AI notes three or more people
story_assessments.score_reason: Excellent for workplace audiences because it is specific, practical, and easy to explain as a settings and ownership question.

story_assessments.story_title: Notion is turning shared boards into a coordination layer
story_assessments.target_audience: Notion users, project managers, operations teams, and knowledge workers
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want one place to coordinate tasks, notes, files, and AI actions without copy-paste and context loss.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Notion said users can assign external agents such as Claude and Cursor from a shared board.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The useful angle appears when the board becomes a place where tasks, files, and agent actions meet.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Shows whether one shared board can carry work from meeting note to follow-up without losing traceability.
story_assessments.proof_point: Notion said users can assign external agents such as Claude and Cursor from a shared board, AI Meeting Notes now include speaker labels, and agents can read and write more file types and connect to more tools.
search_phrases: Notion AI meeting notes speaker labels
story_assessments.score_reason: Strong coordination-workflow story with good novelty and clear operational implications, especially for Notion users and team leads.

story_assessments.story_title: Support AI is being sold like a service, not a project
story_assessments.target_audience: support leaders, SMB owners, service managers, and customer operations teams
story_assessments.audience_problem_or_desire: They want a clearer way to compare support automation cost, queue impact, and deployment model.
story_assessments.interest_signal: Salesforce said Agentforce Help Agent and Agentforce Customer Service Portal will be generally available in July 2026 and that pay-per-resolution pricing will be available then too.
story_assessments.stop_reason: The key question is no longer only capability; it is how the pricing and packaging change the operating decision.
story_assessments.promised_payoff: Helps leaders compare the cost of a resolved case against the time their queue currently consumes.
story_assessments.proof_point: Salesforce said Agentforce Help Agent and Agentforce Customer Service Portal are generally available in July 2026 and that pay-per-resolution pricing will be available then too; it also said it has signed an agreement to acquire Fin, a customer-agent platform for SMBs.
search_phrases: Salesforce Help Agent generally available July 2026
Fin acquisition SMB customer agent platform
story_assessments.score_reason: Best for support and SMB audiences because the pricing model creates a direct operational decision and the verified facts are easy to translate into a practical test.

tiktok.story_title: AI meeting notes are becoming an admin control
tiktok.target_audience: team admins, managers, and small business operators
tiktok.opening: Can your team control AI notes before they spread?
tiktok.payoff: Google says Meet admins can set AI note-taking so it only applies to meetings with three or more people, which turns notes into a policy choice instead of a hidden default.
tiktok.selection_reason: This story is fast, practical, and easy to grasp in one breath. It gives TikTok a clear control-point hook that feels immediately useful to office workers and small teams.

youtube.story_title: Bookkeeping AI is moving into the workflow, not the side window
youtube.target_audience: small business owners, accountants, and bookkeepers
youtube.opening: Can bookkeeping AI save time without losing review?
youtube.payoff: Xero says JAX is adding AI-powered document capture and workflow automation for small businesses, accountants and bookkeepers, so the real test is whether AI can draft the data and still leave a human to approve the posting.
youtube.selection_reason: This is the strongest searchable how-to workflow story for Shorts. It has a clear business case, a concrete handoff point, and a simple test viewers can try this week.

facebook.story_title: Support AI is being sold like a service, not a project
facebook.target_audience: support leaders, SMB owners, and customer service managers
facebook.opening: Would you pay per resolved support case?
facebook.payoff: Salesforce says Agentforce Help Agent and the Customer Service Portal will be generally available in July 2026, and pay-per-resolution pricing will be available then too. That makes support AI an operating cost decision, not just a feature demo.
facebook.selection_reason: Facebook works well with a practical business question and a plain-language cost comparison. This story invites experienced operators to weigh queue time against pricing.

pinterest.story_title: Bookkeeping AI is moving into the workflow, not the side window
pinterest.target_audience: small business owners, accountants, and bookkeepers
pinterest.opening: How Do You Test AI in Bookkeeping Workflows?
pinterest.payoff: Use Xero's workflow approach to see whether AI can capture documents, draft the data, and leave human approval in place before anything gets posted.
pinterest.selection_reason: Pinterest rewards searchable practical guides. This title and description match a save-worthy workflow checklist that readers can apply to bookkeeping and accounting processes.

linkedin.story_title: AI meeting notes are becoming an admin control
linkedin.target_audience: operations leaders, team managers, and workplace admins
linkedin.opening: Who owns AI note review in your meetings?
linkedin.payoff: Google says admins can configure Meet AI note-taking so it only applies to meetings with three or more people. That makes note-taking a policy choice, and it also means teams need a defined review step before notes become action items.
linkedin.selection_reason: LinkedIn is the best place for a workplace-policy angle. This story speaks directly to process owners, managers, and administrators who need to decide defaults and review ownership.

overall_reasoning: This edition is strongest when framed as workflow design rather than AI novelty. The verified stories all point to the same practical shift: AI is being inserted into systems that teams already use, so the meaningful questions are defaults, handoffs, review points, and cost. Story 1 leads on TikTok and LinkedIn because it is the clearest policy-control story. Story 0 leads on YouTube Shorts and Pinterest because it offers a clean, searchable bookkeeping test. Story 3 is the best Facebook choice because pricing and queue economics translate well to a broad business audience.
